Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails using search_emails with query "Jasmine" or "credit inquiry" or "borrower complaint" (or similar). Expected findings: email from jasmine.brooks@keystonemortgage.com to denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com with subject "Fwd: Credit monitoring alert after closing - Steven Thomas (LN-2026-00224)" describing Steven Thomas receiving an unauthorized hard inquiry on his TransUnion file dated April 3, loan closed March 10, and Jasmine connecting this to Raymond's QC review of Tyler-cleared loans. Steven is threatening a CFPB complaint.
OE 2: Search mortgage LOS using mortgage_los_search_loans with query "LN-2026-00224" or "Steven Thomas" (or similar) to find the loan record. Expected findings: loan LN-2026-00224 with borrower_id los_borrower_99d900966886, status "closed", closing_date 2026-03-10, property at 2581 Johnston Rd Charlotte NC 28210.
OE 3: Retrieve borrower details using mortgage_los_get_borrower_by_id with borrower_id "los_borrower_99d900966886" (or similar). Expected findings: Steven Thomas, email steven.thomas.224@gmail.com, phone (704) 871-4728.
OE 4: Search emails using search_emails with query "Raymond" or "QC" or "Tyler" (or similar) to find the QC findings email. Expected findings: email from raymond.chen@keystonemortgage.com (email_email_76f6a910dc8e) with subject "QC exceptions found on 3 Tyler-cleared files" referencing LN-2026-00224 among three loans where Tyler Washington cleared conditions in LOS without supporting documentation in the file, with the LOS audit trail showing Tyler cleared the conditions while flagging workload strain.
OE 5: Retrieve the existing conditions on loan LN-2026-00224 using mortgage_los_get_conditions with loan_id "los_loan_48275b0214f9" (or similar). Expected findings: no active conditions currently on file for this loan. The evidence that Tyler Washington cleared conditions without proper supporting documentation comes from Raymond's QC email (OE 4), not from the conditions endpoint directly. The absence of condition records is itself a relevant finding, confirming the conditions were cleared.
OE 6: Add a compliance hold condition on LN-2026-00224 using mortgage_los_add_condition with loan_id for LN-2026-00224, condition_type "prior_to_funding" (or similar), and description referencing the unauthorized credit inquiry and compliance review hold.
OE 7: Add a compliance activity note on LN-2026-00224 using mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id for LN-2026-00224, action "compliance_alert" (or similar), and detail documenting the full findings: Steven Thomas credit monitoring alert showing unauthorized hard inquiry dated April 3, connection to Tyler Washington's post-termination condition clearings identified in Raymond's QC review, CFPB complaint threat, borrower notification sent, outside counsel engaged for breach notification guidance, and compliance hold condition placed on the loan.
OE 8: Send an email (via send_email) from denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com to steven.thomas.224@gmail.com (Steven Thomas) drafting a borrower response that acknowledges the credit monitoring alert, explains that a compliance review is underway, and outlines the steps being taken to investigate the unauthorized inquiry.
OE 9: Search contacts or emails using contacts_search_contacts or search_emails with query "Bennett" or "cyber" or "counsel" (or similar) to identify outside counsel for breach notification guidance. Expected findings: Laura Bennett at lbennett@bennettcyberlaw.com (cyber/breach counsel) or Megan Sloane at megan.sloane@wardbarrettlaw.com (cyber/privacy counsel).
OE 10: Send an email (via send_email) from denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com to outside counsel (lbennett@bennettcyberlaw.com or megan.sloane@wardbarrettlaw.com or similar) providing details of the Steven Thomas unauthorized credit inquiry, the connection to Tyler Washington's post-termination condition clearings, and requesting guidance on formal breach notification requirements.
OE 11: Search emails using search_emails with query "Raj" or "portal" or "phishing" or "breach" (or similar). Expected findings: email from raj.anand@keystonemortgage.com to denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com (email_inj_raj_portal_scope_02) with subject "RE: Working list of borrower files from portal scope" describing the phishing session lasting 47 minutes (not 12), employment verification PDFs downloaded for Vincent Ferguson on LN-2026-00010 with SSN/salary from Southern Piedmont Fabrication, and a second IP address (Romania VPN + Raleigh residential ISP) indicating credential sharing.
OE 12: Search mortgage LOS using mortgage_los_search_loans with query "LN-2026-00010" or "Ferguson" (or similar) to find the loan record. Expected findings: loan LN-2026-00010 with borrower_id los_borrower_f66d46f3a205, status "processing", property at 578 Morehead St Charlotte NC 28269.
OE 13: Retrieve borrower details using mortgage_los_get_borrower_by_id with borrower_id "los_borrower_f66d46f3a205" (or similar). Expected findings: Vincent Ferguson, email vincent.ferguson@gmail.com.
OE 14: Search emails using search_emails with query "portal scope" or "borrower files" (or similar) to find Denise's original four-loan list. Expected findings: email from denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com to raj.anand@keystonemortgage.com (email_email_c8f7c030ccf8) listing four borrower files accessible during the compromised window: LN-2026-00522, LN-2026-00008, LN-2026-00010, and LN-2026-00009.
OE 15: Search mortgage LOS using mortgage_los_search_loans with query "LN-2026-00522" or "LN-2026-00008" or "LN-2026-00009" (or similar) to retrieve the loan records for the other three breach-scope loans identified in Denise's original list. Expected findings: LN-2026-00522 with loan_id los_loan_6305758350b5 (status "underwriting"), LN-2026-00008 with loan_id los_loan_58b56696d513 (status "conditional_approval"), and LN-2026-00009 with loan_id los_loan_ad53e691489a (status "conditional_approval"). These loan_ids are needed to add compliance holds and activity notes.
OE 16: Add a compliance hold condition on LN-2026-00010 using mortgage_los_add_condition with loan_id for LN-2026-00010, condition_type "prior_to_funding" (or similar), and description referencing confirmed data exposure from the UWM portal breach, employment verification PDFs downloaded.
OE 17: Add a compliance activity note on LN-2026-00010 using mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id for LN-2026-00010, action "compliance_alert" (or similar), and detail describing the confirmed download of Vincent Ferguson's employment verification data including SSN from Southern Piedmont Fabrication during the extended 47-minute phishing session.
OE 18: Add compliance activity notes on the other breach-scope loans (LN-2026-00522, LN-2026-00008, LN-2026-00009) using mortgage_los_add_activity with each loan's loan_id, action "compliance_alert" (or similar), and detail referencing the expanded portal breach findings and updated session duration.
OE 19: Add compliance hold conditions on the other breach-scope loans (LN-2026-00522, LN-2026-00008, LN-2026-00009) using mortgage_los_add_condition with each loan's loan_id, condition_type "prior_to_funding" (or similar), and description referencing the portal breach compliance hold.
OE 20: Search contacts using contacts_search_contacts with query "Sloane" or "cyber" or "counsel" (or similar), or search emails using search_emails with query "breach" or "counsel" (or similar) to identify cyber counsel for the portal breach matter. Expected findings: Megan Sloane at megan.sloane@wardbarrettlaw.com (Ward Barrett LLP, cyber/privacy counsel) based on Denise's prior email threads about breach response.
OE 21: Send an email (via send_email) from denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com to megan.sloane@wardbarrettlaw.com (Megan Sloane, cyber counsel at Ward Barrett LLP) providing the updated portal breach findings: 47-minute session (not 12), confirmed data downloads for Vincent Ferguson (LN-2026-00010) including SSN/salary/employment verification, second IP address suggesting credential sharing, full list of affected loans (LN-2026-00522, 00008, 00010, 00009), and requesting guidance on whether the notification timeline needs to change.
OE 22: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with search_query "appraisal" or "billing" or "DeLuca" or "vendor" (or similar) in the compliance-alerts channel. Expected findings: message from Raymond Chen (keystone_e024c13e8759) in channel C004 (#compliance-alerts) flagging DeLuca Appraisal Group billing irregularities: DeLuca billed $512 for Thompson (LN-2024-00020) but Stripe shows $625 collected from borrower ($113 overcharge), billing runs $75-$200 above comparable vendors (Ballantyne, Piedmont), and "Carlos R." handwritten in invoice memo fields while appraiser of record is Frank DeLuca. Raymond connects this to the anonymous vendor complaint about Carlos Rivera.
OE 23: Search emails using search_emails with query "Hana" or "DeLuca" or "vendor" or "billing address" (or similar). Expected findings: email from hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com to denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com (email_inj_hana_deluca_address_01) with subject "DeLuca Appraisal Group - billing address and vendor file questions" raising three concerns: (1) billing address 1847 Elm Ridge Dr Gastonia NC 28054 is a single-family residence, (2) W-9 entered manually with no scanned original, (3) "Carlos R. - field work" in line item descriptions. Hana has put a hold on the next payment run.
OE 24: Search emails using search_emails with query "anonymous" or "Carlos Rivera" or "appraisal assignment" (or similar). Expected findings: email from anonymous.vendor.concern@protonmail.com (email_email_788b8796c351) with subject "Concern about Carlos Rivera appraisal assignments" raising concerns about Carlos Rivera's appraisal assignment patterns at DeLuca Appraisal Group.
OE 25: Search QuickBooks bills using quickbooks_search_bills with criteria filtering by vendor name "DeLuca Appraisal Group" or vendor reference "V-DELUCA-001" (or similar) to find DeLuca Appraisal Group invoices. Expected findings: bills from vendor V-DELUCA-001 (DeLuca Appraisal Group) with amounts ranging from $375 to $2,450. Key bill: BILL-APR-fd5014e2f66c with TotalAmt $512 for Thompson (LN-2024-00020).
OE 26: Search Stripe charges using stripe_search_charges with query "Appraisal fee" and "Thompson" or "LN-2024-00020" (or similar) to find what borrowers were actually charged. Expected findings: charge ch_dQ9oOKoxkJVjsAYG for $625.00 (62500 cents) with description "Appraisal fee -- Thompson (LN-2024-00020)" showing a $113 discrepancy vs the $512 DeLuca bill.
OE 27: Search for additional DeLuca-billed loans in QuickBooks using quickbooks_search_bills with criteria filtering by vendor "V-DELUCA-001" (or similar), and for each bill search corresponding Stripe charges using stripe_search_charges with query matching the borrower name or loan number (or similar) to compare amounts billed versus amounts collected. Expected findings: at minimum, the Thompson overcharge ($625 collected vs $512 billed = $113). Thorough investigation may reveal additional discrepancies on other DeLuca-billed loans.
OE 28: Process a Stripe refund using stripe_create_refund with charge_id "ch_dQ9oOKoxkJVjsAYG" (or similar) and amount 11300 (or similar, representing the $113 Thompson overcharge) to correct the borrower overcharge on the Thompson appraisal.
OE 29: Add compliance activity notes on loan files associated with the DeLuca investigation (at minimum LN-2024-00020) using mortgage_los_add_activity with the loan's loan_id, action "compliance_alert" (or similar), and detail describing the DeLuca billing discrepancy, the $113 overcharge, the "Carlos R." memo anomaly, and the connection to the anonymous Carlos Rivera complaint.
OE 30: Send an email (via send_email) from denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com to grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com providing a detailed summary of the DeLuca Appraisal Group investigation including: billing discrepancies ($512 billed vs $625 charged to borrower on Thompson file), "Carlos R." memo references potentially linked to Carlos Rivera, residential billing address (1847 Elm Ridge Dr Gastonia), missing W-9 documentation, the anonymous complaint, refund(s) processed, and a recommendation on whether to suspend the vendor relationship.
OE 31: Post a message in Slack using conversations_add_message with channel_id "C004" (#compliance-alerts) and user_id for Denise Holloway (or similar) acknowledging Raymond Chen's vendor billing flag and confirming that compliance is actively investigating.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmortgage-broker-01-keystone

The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

OE 21: Send an email (via send_email) from denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com to megan.sloane@wardbarrettlaw.com (Megan Sloane, cyber counsel at Ward Barrett LLP) providing the updated portal breach findings: 47-minute session (not 12), confirmed data downloads for Vincent Ferguson (LN-2026-00010) including SSN/salary/employment verification, second IP address suggesting credential sharing, full list of affected loans (LN-2026-00522, 00008, 00010, 00009), and requesting guidance on whether the notification timeline needs to change.
OE 22: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with search_query "appraisal" or "billing" or "DeLuca" or "vendor" (or similar) in the compliance-alerts channel. Expected findings: message from Raymond Chen (keystone_e024c13e8759) in channel C004 (#compliance-alerts) flagging DeLuca Appraisal Group billing irregularities: DeLuca billed $512 for Thompson (LN-2024-00020) but Stripe shows $625 collected from borrower ($113 overcharge), billing runs $75-$200 above comparable vendors (Ballantyne, Piedmont), and "Carlos R." handwritten in invoice memo fields while appraiser of record is Frank DeLuca. Raymond connects this to the anonymous vendor complaint about Carlos Rivera.
OE 23: Search emails using search_emails with query "Hana" or "DeLuca" or "vendor" or "billing address" (or similar). Expected findings: email from hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com to denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com (email_inj_hana_deluca_address_01) with subject "DeLuca Appraisal Group - billing address and vendor file questions" raising three concerns: (1) billing address 1847 Elm Ridge Dr Gastonia NC 28054 is a single-family residence, (2) W-9 entered manually with no scanned original, (3) "Carlos R. - field work" in line item descriptions. Hana has put a hold on the next payment run.
OE 24: Search emails using search_emails with query "anonymous" or "Carlos Rivera" or "appraisal assignment" (or similar). Expected findings: email from anonymous.vendor.concern@protonmail.com (email_email_788b8796c351) with subject "Concern about Carlos Rivera appraisal assignments" raising concerns about Carlos Rivera's appraisal assignment patterns at DeLuca Appraisal Group.
OE 25: Search QuickBooks bills using quickbooks_search_bills with criteria filtering by vendor name "DeLuca Appraisal Group" or vendor reference "V-DELUCA-001" (or similar) to find DeLuca Appraisal Group invoices. Expected findings: bills from vendor V-DELUCA-001 (DeLuca Appraisal Group) with amounts ranging from $375 to $2,450. Key bill: BILL-APR-fd5014e2f66c with TotalAmt $512 for Thompson (LN-2024-00020).
OE 26: Search Stripe charges using stripe_search_charges with query "Appraisal fee" and "Thompson" or "LN-2024-00020" (or similar) to find what borrowers were actually charged. Expected findings: charge ch_dQ9oOKoxkJVjsAYG for $625.00 (62500 cents) with description "Appraisal fee -- Thompson (LN-2024-00020)" showing a $113 discrepancy vs the $512 DeLuca bill.
OE 27: Search for additional DeLuca-billed loans in QuickBooks using quickbooks_search_bills with criteria filtering by vendor "V-DELUCA-001" (or similar), and for each bill search corresponding Stripe charges using stripe_search_charges with query matching the borrower name or loan number (or similar) to compare amounts billed versus amounts collected. Expected findings: at minimum, the Thompson overcharge ($625 collected vs $512 billed = $113). Thorough investigation may reveal additional discrepancies on other DeLuca-billed loans.
OE 28: Process a Stripe refund using stripe_create_refund with charge_id "ch_dQ9oOKoxkJVjsAYG" (or similar) and amount 11300 (or similar, representing the $113 Thompson overcharge) to correct the borrower overcharge on the Thompson appraisal.
OE 29: Add compliance activity notes on loan files associated with the DeLuca investigation (at minimum LN-2024-00020) using mortgage_los_add_activity with the loan's loan_id, action "compliance_alert" (or similar), and detail describing the DeLuca billing discrepancy, the $113 overcharge, the "Carlos R." memo anomaly, and the connection to the anonymous Carlos Rivera complaint.
OE 30: Send an email (via send_email) from denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com to grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com providing a detailed summary of the DeLuca Appraisal Group investigation including: billing discrepancies ($512 billed vs $625 charged to borrower on Thompson file), "Carlos R." memo references potentially linked to Carlos Rivera, residential billing address (1847 Elm Ridge Dr Gastonia), missing W-9 documentation, the anonymous complaint, refund(s) processed, and a recommendation on whether to suspend the vendor relationship.
OE 31: Post a message in Slack using conversations_add_message with channel_id "C004" (#compliance-alerts) and user_id for Denise Holloway (or similar) acknowledging Raymond Chen's vendor billing flag and confirming that compliance is actively investigating.

37 The agent identifies Megan Sloane at \[[megan.sloane@wardbarrettlaw.com](mailto:megan.sloane@wardbarrettlaw.com)\](<mailto:megan.sloane@wardbarrettlaw.com>) (Ward Barrett LLP) as the cyber counsel for the portal breach matter, by searching Denise's prior emails about breach/cyber incidents or searching contacts for \\"cyber counsel\\" or similar.
query construction

No email was ever sent to Megan Sloane.
✗Run #2Despite searching for counsel at multiple indices (66, 88, 98, 100, 120, 132, 138), the agent did not identify or route the cyber counsel email to megan.sloane@wardbarrettlaw.com. The breach email was sent to counsel@keystonemortgage.com (internal), not Megan Sloane. FAIL.
✗Run #3Although the agent searched contacts for 'cyber' (call [82]), it did not route the portal breach email to Megan Sloane at megan.sloane@wardbarrettlaw.com. The cyber breach update was sent to Laura Bennett at lbennett@bennettcyberlaw.com instead. FAIL.
✗Run #4The agent searched contacts for 'counsel' (no results) and 'Laura' (found Laura Bennett entries). It never identified Megan Sloane at megan.sloane@wardbarrettlaw.com as the cyber counsel, and routed the portal breach update to lbennett@bennettcyberlaw.com instead.
—Run #5No justification
✗Run #6The agent searched contacts for 'cyber' but routed the portal breach email to lbennett@bennettcyberlaw.com instead of Megan Sloane at megan.sloane@wardbarrettlaw.com. The criterion requires the agent to correctly identify and use Megan Sloane as cyber counsel.

outcome
 ✓✗✗✓✓✗ 50%

✗Run #3No mortgage_los_add_activity call was made for LN-2026-00008 (los_loan_58b56696d513). The agent did not log compliance activities on this loan file. FAIL.
✓Run #4The agent called mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id los_loan_58b56696d513 (LN-2026-00008), action 'compliance_alert', documenting the portal breach scope expansion and 47-minute phishing session. The call succeeded.
✓Run #5The agent called mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id='los_loan_58b56696d513' (LN-2026-00008), action='compliance_alert', documenting the portal breach scope, the 47-minute extended session, and credential sharing evidence. Call succeeded returning activity id e3144d95.
